key_evidence: A haul truck operator died at the Morenci mine on 2026-07-06 (MSHA preliminary report) — the third fatality tied to Morenci since 2021.
Seven contract workers were killed by an 800,000-tonne mud rush at the Grasberg mine, Central Papua, on 2025-09-08; bodies recovered 2025-10-05.
IndustriALL reports unions have repeatedly reported mine accidents and workers' rights violations at Grasberg over the past decade.
Countervailing structure: third-party human rights impact assessments, ICMM validation against 38 performance expectations, and Copper Mark assurance.
SCREENING RESULT: entity surfaced on negative evidence; the +13.7 gap is a placeholder artifact, not new positive conduct. Routed to calibration review, NOT proposed as an upgrade.

evidence.quote: A haul truck operator died when the truck he was operating collided with another haul truck.
evidence.source: US Mine Safety and Health Administration
evidence.claim: Supports the fatality record and the countervailing third-party assurance infrastructure.

evidence.quote: The workers had been trapped underground following a landslide on 8 September, when an estimated 800,000 metric tonnes of mud suddenly flowed into the cave.
evidence.claim: Supports the fatality record and the countervailing third-party assurance infrastructure.

evidence.claim: Supports the fatality record and the countervailing third-party assurance infrastructure.

calibration_note: DO NOT APPLY THIS AS A ONE-OFF UPGRADE. Freeport-McMoRan has never been individually assessed; the published 18.8 is a bulk-import placeholder. The first full 40-subdimension review finds materially more compassion infrastructure than the placeholder assumed — third-party human rights impact assessments, ICMM Mining Principles validation against 38 performance expectations, and Copper Mark assurance. That produces a +13.7 gap. But the anti-false-positive screen (agent definition section 3e-bis) governs here, and this proposal deliberately does NOT clear it. Freeport-McMoRan was surfaced on NEGATIVE within-window evidence: a worker killed at the Morenci mine on 2026-07-06. There is no new within-window evidence of positive, countervailing conduct by the company. Under screening rule 3, negative-trigger evidence must not yield an upgrade, and under rule 5, a belief that a published score is mis-calibrated must be routed to calibration review rather than encoded as a one-off reset. The recommendation is therefore flag-for-review, not upgrade. The correct remedy is a band-wide calibration review of never-assessed Fortune 500 entities sitting in the Critical band, not an isolated move on this one company while it is under active federal fatality investigation.
